parentQuestion: The lipid bilayer is primarily composed of molecules called ___.

options: A lipid with a phosphate group that forms cell membranes
scaffolds.explain: A PHOSPHOLIPID is a LIPID with a PHOSPHATE GROUP that forms cell membranes. It has a hydrophilic head and hydrophobic tails.

wrongExplains.0: Proteins are made of amino acids; phospholipids are lipids.
wrongExplains.2: Carbohydrates are sugars; phospholipids are lipids.
wrongExplains.3: Nucleic acids are DNA/RNA; phospholipids are lipids.

options: Having both water-loving and water-fearing parts
scaffolds.explain: AMPHIPATHIC means having BOTH water-loving (hydrophilic) AND water-fearing (hydrophobic) parts. This property allows phospholipids to form bilayers.

wrongExplains.0: Completely water-loving is hydrophilic; amphipathic has both.
wrongExplains.2: Completely water-fearing is hydrophobic; amphipathic has both.
wrongExplains.3: Amphipathic refers to water interaction, not charge.

scaffolds.explain: HYDROPHILIC means WATER-LOVING. Hydrophilic molecules dissolve in or interact well with water.

wrongExplains.0: Hydrophobic is water-fearing; hydrophilic is water-loving.
wrongExplains.2: Lipophilic is fat-loving; hydrophilic is water-loving.
wrongExplains.3: Hydrophilic specifically means attracted to water.

scaffolds.explain: HYDROPHOBIC means WATER-FEARING. Hydrophobic molecules repel water and dissolve in fats/oils.

wrongExplains.0: Hydrophilic is water-loving; hydrophobic is water-fearing.
wrongExplains.2: Hydrophobic molecules are actually fat-loving, not fat-fearing.
wrongExplains.3: Hydrophobic refers to water repulsion, not charge.

scaffolds.q: How many layers of lipids are in a cell membrane?
scaffolds.explain: Cell membranes have TWO layers (BILAYER). The hydrophobic tails face inward, hydrophilic heads face outward.

wrongExplains.0: One layer couldn't form a stable barrier; two are needed.
wrongExplains.2: Cell membranes have two layers, not three.
wrongExplains.3: Cell membranes have two layers, not four.

scaffolds.q: How are phospholipids arranged in the bilayer?
options: Hydrophobic tails facing each other, hydrophilic heads facing outward
scaffolds.explain: Phospholipids arrange with HYDROPHOBIC TAILS FACING EACH OTHER and HYDROPHILIC HEADS FACING OUTWARD toward water.

wrongExplains.0: Heads face water (outward); tails avoid water (inward).
wrongExplains.2: Arrangement is highly organized, not random.
wrongExplains.3: Two opposing layers face each other.

scaffolds.q: Why do phospholipids spontaneously form bilayers?
options: Their amphipathic nature drives them to hide tails from water
scaffolds.explain: Phospholipids SPONTANEOUSLY form bilayers because their AMPHIPATHIC NATURE drives hydrophobic tails to hide from water.

wrongExplains.0: Bilayer formation is spontaneous, not enzyme-driven.
wrongExplains.2: Self-assembly is driven by water avoidance, not protein attraction.
wrongExplains.3: Bilayer formation is spontaneous, requiring no ATP.

scaffolds.q: What is the fluid mosaic model?
options: A model describing the cell membrane as fluid with embedded proteins
scaffolds.explain: The FLUID MOSAIC MODEL describes the cell membrane as FLUID (phospholipids move) with embedded proteins creating a MOSAIC pattern.

wrongExplains.0: DNA structure is the double helix model.

scaffolds.q: The lipid bilayer is primarily composed of molecules called ___?
scaffolds.explain: The lipid bilayer is primarily composed of PHOSPHOLIPIDS — amphipathic molecules that arrange in two layers.

wrongExplains.0: Proteins are embedded in the membrane but don't form the bilayer.
wrongExplains.2: Carbohydrates are on the surface but don't form the bilayer.
wrongExplains.3: Nucleic acids are in the nucleus, not the membrane.
